Inkling ranks 8th on the Agentic Web Dev leaderboard with a score of 1257, highlighted among various model scores.
my chisel laser cutting program in the browser
laser cut plywood

Generalist model
Inkling is designed to be broad. We trained it across agentic, reasoning, coding, instruction-following, factuality, vision, and audio tasks, rather than narrowly optimizing for one domain. That breadth matters for customization and real-world use: different users need models that can adapt to very different workflows, not just excel on benchmarks.

the type annotation is `{a b} (a -> b) -> Maybe a -> Maybe b`

And the cardboard — so close: corrugated boxes are actually 1890s, a Brooklyn printer named Robert Gair invented the die-cut carton by accident when a press rule slipped and cut instead of creased. But their conquest — killing the wooden crate and the barrel as the default way things move — yes, that's postwar, containerization era. So barrels ruled because they rolled, and boxes won because they stack. The extra hands, though — that one I can't back you on. People had the regular number of hands. They just had sons.

Each attachment is a model-theoretic posit whose own status is contingent (there are models with it and without it; models with different orders). Its non- necessity is expressible, and the analysis iterates in K/ T/S4/B. So the "home" is always a construction, and constructions are empty of svabhāva. Nice.
